preview_text:
The model favors Mexico with a 71% win probability, leaving Czech Republic at just 7%. This represents a substantial confidence gap, with the model assigning the underdog only one-tenth the winning chance of the favorite.

The forecast structure reflects a heavily tilted matchup. Mexico's draw probability sits at 23%, suggesting the model sees meaningful risk of a stalemate but otherwise expects a decisive outcome. Czech Republic's 7% win chance indicates the model rates them as unlikely to prevail even in a competitive contest. The narrow space for an upset aligns with the wide gap in outright win probabilities.

Model and market diverge materially here. The sportsbook consensus cuts Mexico's win probability to 52%, roughly 19 percentage points below the model, while elevating Czech Republic to 21%—nearly triple the model's assessment. Polymarket prices align closer to the books than to the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence suggests meaningful disagreement on the underlying competitive balance.
